title: Support Enter Key for Rapid Metric Name Entry
story: As a DataTools Pro user entering metrics, I want to press Enter to submit a metric name instead of clicking the save button, so that I can rapidly input multiple metrics without switching between keyboard and mouse.
description: When the metrics name text box is focused and contains at least one character, pressing Enter now triggers the same save action as clicking the save button. After a successful save, the cursor automatically returns to the text box so the user can immediately type the next metric name. This keyboard-driven workflow eliminates the need to click the mouse between entries, enabling rapid sequential metric input. The same auto-focus behavior was also applied when the save button is clicked.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing Enter in the metrics name text box triggers the save action when at least one character is entered
Pressing Enter with an empty text box does not trigger the save action
After successful save via Enter key, the text box is cleared and re-focused for the next entry
After successful save via button click, the text box is cleared and re-focused
Default form submission behavior is prevented when Enter is pressed
ux_changes: Added keypress listener on the metrics name input for Enter key. After save (via Enter or button click), cursor auto-returns to the text box for rapid sequential entry.
implementation_notes: Implementation uses jQuery keypress event listener on #metrics_name checking for keyCode 13 (Enter). Prevents default form submission, validates non-empty input, triggers the save button click, and re-focuses the input. Toast notification (MBT-156) was planned as a follow-up.
comment_summary: Product owner provided the jQuery implementation code. Developer implemented it and extended the auto-focus behavior to also work on button clicks. Toast feedback was deferred to a separate ticket.
